1-321: A warning sign is usually placed about 150 m before the hazard, so you have enough time to react.
6-460: Auxiliary devices count as official traffic signs, and they complete the meaning of the sign they accompany.
6-461: When there are arrows above the traffic light, you follow the arrows in all cases — they control your direction of travel.
6-468: A flashing green light warns that the light is about to turn yellow, then red — get ready to stop.
6-472: If there is no stop line and no pedestrian crossing, you stop beside the nearest pole carrying the traffic light.
7-482: A driving-school vehicle is allowed to enter the expressway during instruction.
7-483: A triangle-shaped sign is a warning sign — it alerts you to a hazard ahead.
7-501: Lines and markings painted on the road are binding exactly like signs, in all cases.
7-502: A continuous dividing line forbids overtaking, so you stay behind the cart at a slow speed until conditions allow.
8-521: Angled parking is forbidden unless road markings allow it — without them, you park parallel to the curb.
8-522: On an uphill road the ascending vehicle has priority, but it is always wise to be considerate of the truck.
8-523: When approaching a railway crossing you must slow down in all cases and make sure before you cross.
8-524: Picking up and dropping off passengers on the road shoulder is allowed, except on expressways — there it is dangerous.
8-525: A hand signal is used when the signaling devices (indicators) break down, so you can keep signaling to those around you.
8-526: Legally, night starts a quarter of an hour after sunset and ends a quarter of an hour before sunrise.
8-527: A vehicle with a minor fault may stay parked on the road for a maximum of 24 hours — after that it must move.
8-528: Order of obedience: the police officer first, then the traffic light (except flashing yellow), then the sign, then the law.
8-529: The dimmer switches between the high (road) beam and the low (dipped) beam and back.
8-530: If an accident causes injury or death, stop immediately where you are and never move the vehicle — it is an accident scene.
8-531: You recognize an expressway by the 'Entering an expressway' sign — that is the official marker.
8-532: Turning left from a two-way road into a two-way road: from as close as possible to the center, into the left lane of your direction.
8-533: When you see an animal on the road, slow down — sudden movement or the horn could scare it and cause danger.
8-534: If the barrier at a railway crossing inside the city is open, there is no duty to stop — an open barrier means it is safe.
8-535: Any driver parked at the roadside who wants to move off must signal, even without changing lanes — to alert those behind.
8-536: Lighting-up time: night, plus any time visibility is poor (fog, rain...) — that is when you switch the lights on.
8-537: The stop line is a line drawn across the road that marks where the vehicle must stop when stopping is required.
8-538: If the accident caused property damage only, you must clear the road if the vehicle is blocking traffic.
8-539: Near schools, playgrounds, and children, slow down and increase your caution — children move unpredictably.
9-540: A road worker's signals are binding when they use the 'stop' and 'go' signals at the barrier.
9-541: On a very steep road, keep a bigger distance from the vehicle ahead in all cases — braking takes longer.
9-542: Turning right from a one-way road into a one-way road: from the far right into the far right — a tight arc.
9-543: On a downhill road, priority goes to the ascending vehicle if circumstances require one of them to stop.
9-544: Never refuel while the engine is running — fire hazard. Switch the engine off first.
9-545: The high (road) beam lights up a distance of at least 100 m in front of the vehicle.
9-546: The low (dipped) beam lights up a distance of at least 30 m in front of the vehicle.
9-547: The reversing light illuminates no more than 10 m behind the vehicle.
9-548: Repairing a vehicle on the road is allowed under all the conditions mentioned (all the answers are correct).
9-549: The warning lamp in front of the driver lights up in blue when the high beam is on.
9-550: Turning left into a one-way road: you enter on the left side of the road in every case.
9-551: Parking on the sidewalk is forbidden unless a sign allows it — the sidewalk is for pedestrians.
9-552: Overtaking is allowed only when the road conditions and traffic allow it — enough visibility and space.
9-553: The road must be used in a way that does not obstruct traffic and does not endanger anyone.
9-554: The warning triangle is placed behind the vehicle so a driver coming from behind can see it from at least 100 m.
9-555: A lane is a part of the road width wide enough for one line of vehicles traveling on more than two wheels.
9-556: A median (dividing area) is any structure or traffic island dividing the road along its length.
9-557: Parking is forbidden within 12 m before a stop line.
9-558: If a vehicle stopped before a pedestrian crossing to fulfill its duty, you may not overtake it — there may be pedestrians you cannot see.
9-559: When parking beside the curb, the distance between the curb and the wheels must not exceed 40 cm.
9-560: When exiting a parking area onto the road, give right of way to the crossing traffic — you are the one entering.
9-561: When parking on a descent, turn the steering wheel toward the curb/road edge — so if the car rolls, it stops against the curb.
9-562: The person legally responsible for passengers wearing seat belts is the driver.
9-563: Overtaking is forbidden if it would obstruct the other vehicle or put it in danger.
9-564: On a narrow road that cannot fit two vehicles, both move to the far right, even down onto the road shoulder.
9-565: The warning triangle is placed in every case where the vehicle stops because of a fault — day or night.
9-566: On an uphill road with a curb, point the wheels toward the center of the road when parking.
9-567: Parking and stopping are forbidden within the limits of a bus stop.
9-568: Driving on the sidewalk is allowed only to enter or exit a yard/square — briefly and carefully.
9-569: Turning left on a road with a continuous dividing line: from as close as possible to the continuous line.
10-570: Crossing the median is absolutely forbidden — it is a fixed separator between the two directions of traffic.
10-571: A police officer's order overrides everything — even if the road is closed, if the officer tells you to enter, you enter.
10-572: Every road user (driver, pedestrian, cyclist...) must act with caution — not only drivers.
10-573: Parking on the left side is allowed only on a one-way road marked with a sign at its entrance.
10-574: To avoid hitting the vehicle in front of you, keep a sufficient safety distance between you.
10-575: Driving under the influence of sedative drugs is absolutely forbidden — they affect your focus and reaction time.
10-576: A one-way road: traffic moves in one direction, or it is the road running alongside a median.
10-577: A hand signal is allowed if the vehicle's indicators break down.
10-578: On a two-way road divided into three lanes, the middle lane is used for overtaking only.
10-579: On an uphill road without a curb, point the wheels toward the road edge when parking.
10-580: At an intersection, priority goes to the vehicle coming from the right, when no signs say otherwise.
10-581: Turning left (one lane per direction): from as close as possible to the center, in a wide arc to the right side of the intended road.
10-582: Slow down when passing a bus stopped at a station — passengers may step out suddenly.
10-583: When visibility is limited, overtaking is forbidden — you cannot see what is coming toward you.
10-584: On a descent that cannot fit two vehicles, priority goes to the ascending vehicles.
10-585: The horn is used to prevent a danger that cannot be avoided any other way — not for annoyance.
10-586: When an emergency vehicle approaches, move your vehicle to the right, stop, and give it right of way.
10-587: After giving way to an emergency vehicle, you may not follow it until it is at least 100 m ahead.
10-588: A broken line in the middle of the road allows overtaking when the road is clear.
10-589: At an intersection without signs, priority goes to the vehicle coming from the right and approaching the intersection.
10-590: A road closed at both ends is still considered a road in the eyes of the law.
10-591: On a descent with a curb, point the wheels toward the curb and use reverse gear.
10-592: Near a group of children, slow down and increase your caution — their movements are sudden.
10-593: If two vehicles collide with no injuries, move the vehicles away and clear any hazardous material off the road.
10-594: When moving off from the side of the road, give priority to vehicles coming from behind in your direction.
10-595: Slow down before a narrow bridge — a vehicle may come from the other direction and space is tight.
10-596: The road shoulder extends up to 3 m from the road edge, or to the drainage channel.
10-597: When a median divides the whole road into two parts, each part becomes a one-way road.
10-598: The high beam is used outside the cities, and inside the cities when the road is not lit well enough.
10-599: When reversing, you must take every measure to prevent danger (all the answers are correct).
11-600: On a one-way road with more than two lanes: drive on the right, use the left lane to overtake or the far left to turn left.
11-601: The handbrake is used to secure the vehicle when parked, or in emergencies.
11-602: When there are arrows on the road for several directions, you may move between lanes after taking precautions.
11-603: The indicator's job is signaling when you leave your lane — to alert those around you.
11-604: A mobile machine is a vehicle built to carry out works, not built for towing.
11-605: When parking beside the curb, the distance between the wheels and the curb must not exceed 40 cm.
11-606: Your driving license must be with you at all times while driving.
11-607: On a non-urban road, stopping on the shoulder to pick up/drop off passengers is allowed — except on expressways.
11-608: On a steep descent, keep a bigger distance from the vehicle ahead in all cases.
11-609: The warning triangle is placed behind the vehicle whenever it has a fault and stops for any repair.
11-610: To prevent a collision with the vehicle ahead, keep a sufficient distance between you.
11-611: If your lane is closed, give right of way to vehicles in the other lanes in all cases.
11-612: You must always give right of way to emergency vehicles.
11-613: Turning left from a one-way road into a one-way road: from the far left into the far left.
11-614: A U-turn to travel in the opposite direction is allowed under all the conditions mentioned (all the answers are correct).
11-615: Parking is forbidden within 20 m of a railway crossing.
11-616: The reversing light is used only when reversing.
11-617: A road user is everyone who uses the road for traveling, walking, or standing.
11-618: Slanted white lines (a traffic island) mark an area where driving and parking are forbidden.
11-619: At a 'Stop' sign at an intersection, stop at a place from which you can see the traffic.
11-620: On a long, steep descent, slow down until the end of the descent — to keep control of the vehicle.
11-621: The vehicle being overtaken must not speed up and must keep to the far right — to make overtaking easier.
11-622: To avoid hitting the vehicle ahead if it stops suddenly, always keep a sufficient distance.
11-623: On a rainy day, keep a bigger distance than usual — the road is slippery and braking takes longer.
11-624: Before and through a bend, drive slowly — to keep control of the vehicle.
11-625: Behind any vehicle, keep a distance sufficient to stop and prevent an accident.
11-626: On an uphill road with a curb: first gear, wheels toward the center of the road, with the handbrake.
11-627: The faster you drive, the bigger the distance you must keep from the vehicle ahead.
11-628: When parking on an uphill road, use first (forward) gear.
11-629: Near a pedestrian crossing, let pedestrians cross and stop when necessary.
12-630: Reversing is allowed only when necessary — visibility is limited and it is dangerous.
12-631: The distance from the vehicle ahead takes into account your speed, the road, and the vehicle's condition (all the answers are correct).
12-632: On a road with more than two lanes, drive in the right lane except when overtaking.
12-633: Overtaking a motorcycle is allowed when the road is clear.
12-634: On an uphill road, overtaking is forbidden when the field of vision is limited.
12-635: When passing a bus stopped at a station, slow down even if that slightly delays traffic — passengers may step out.
12-636: Alongside a bus stopped at a station, slow down in all cases.
12-637: If your lane is closed, give priority to vehicles in the other lanes in all cases.
12-638: If the right headlight burns out at night, you may not continue with one light — it is dangerous.
12-639: A broken dividing line allows overtaking when the road is clear.
12-640: When there are two lanes in your direction, drive in the right one except when overtaking.
12-641: On a narrow road with an oncoming vehicle: slow down and keep to the far right, even onto the road shoulder.
12-642: The red light in the traffic signal is binding on the driver — you must stop.
12-643: Driving on the median is forbidden.
12-644: The law requires driving at the far right in all cases except when overtaking.
12-645: When overtaking, signal before moving into the left lane.
12-646: If you are stopped at the traffic light and want to turn right, you must signal with the indicator.
12-647: If there is an obstacle (pole/island) on a two-way road, pass it on the right unless a sign says otherwise.
12-648: On a downhill road, priority goes to the ascending vehicle when two vehicles cannot pass.
12-649: Parking on an uphill road beside the sidewalk includes all the measures mentioned (all the answers are correct).
12-650: If the vehicle ahead signaled it wants to turn left, you may pass it on its right side.
12-651: You must use the indicator when you intend to move your vehicle off — to alert those around you.
12-652: Keep a reasonable distance from the vehicle ahead in all cases.
12-653: When you speed up behind a vehicle, you must increase the distance between you.
12-654: Inside the cities, keep a distance sufficient to stop the vehicle at any moment and prevent an accident.
12-655: In a convoy outside the cities, keep a distance big enough for another vehicle to fit between you and the one ahead.
12-656: If an oncoming vehicle appears at night while you are on high beam, dip the lights the moment you see it.
12-657: An island marked with slanted white lines means an area you may not drive on.
12-658: Angled parking beside the curb is allowed only where special road markings permit it.
12-659: When an emergency vehicle approaches, slow down, stop on the right, and give it priority.
13-660: Slow down or stop — even within the legal speed — when meeting animals on the road.
13-661: Slow down or stop on a long, steep descent — to keep control of the vehicle.
13-662: Slow down or stop near a group of children or a crowd.
13-663: Passing on the right is allowed when the vehicle ahead wants to turn left, has signaled, and the road is wide enough.
13-664: Near schools and playgrounds the risk to children is higher, so slow down and increase your caution.
13-665: At an intersection, priority goes to the vehicle from the right in all cases when no sign requires otherwise.
13-666: When turning left at an intersection, give priority to oncoming traffic in all cases.
13-667: A continuous dividing line forbids overtaking, so you stay behind the cart at a slow speed — overtaking it is forbidden.
13-668: At an intersection without signs, slow down and give priority to vehicles from the right near the intersection.
13-669: Turning left from one-way into two-way: from the left side, in a wide arc, to the far right of the intended road.
13-670: On a road with two or more lanes, drive in the right lane except when overtaking.
13-671: On a downhill road, give priority to ascending vehicles if the road cannot fit two vehicles.
13-672: If a vehicle is stopped to fulfill its duty before a railway crossing, overtaking it is absolutely forbidden.
13-673: Overtaking a motorcycle is allowed only when the road is clear.
13-674: Overtaking before a crest on an uphill road is forbidden because the field of vision is limited.
13-675: Inside an intersection with one lane per direction, overtaking is absolutely forbidden.
13-676: When the barrier is open at a railway crossing, slow down, make sure there is no danger, then cross.
13-677: Sudden stopping is allowed only when there is no other way to prevent an accident.
13-678: At an intersection, overtaking is forbidden unless there are two lanes in your direction of travel.
13-679: At an intersection without signs, give priority to the vehicle coming from the right.
13-680: When exiting a fuel station onto the public road, give priority to all vehicles on the road.
13-681: Overtaking before a pedestrian crossing is forbidden in all cases — there may be hidden pedestrians.
13-682: On an urban road without a speed sign, the maximum speed for a private car is 50 km/h.
13-683: On a two-way road with three lanes, drive in the right lane except when overtaking or turning left.
13-684: A traffic island without a sign is always passed on the right, unless a sign says otherwise.
13-685: In a private car outside the cities, driving faster than 80 km/h is forbidden.
13-686: On a two-way road with a continuous dividing line, stopping and parking are absolutely forbidden.
13-687: At night on a non-urban road with an oncoming vehicle, dip your lights immediately so you don't dazzle the driver.
13-688: If the vehicle ahead wants to turn left and has signaled, you may pass it on the right carefully if the road is wide enough.
13-689: In a dangerous situation (children / pedestrian crossing), slow down — safety comes before speed.
14-690: When you notice an animal on the road, slow down.
14-691: Near a narrow bridge, slow down when approaching and while crossing.
14-692: Give right of way to emergency vehicles in all cases.
14-693: A 'Stop' sign on the right of the intersection requires you to stop in all cases.
14-694: Signal from a reasonable distance before attempting to deviate or turn — to alert others.
14-695: Signal to other drivers when you want to deviate from your lane.
14-696: Turning left into a one-way road that has a median: you enter on the left side of the intended road.
14-697: On a narrow road that cannot fit two vehicles, both move to the far right, even onto the road margin.
14-698: When lanes have arrows for several directions, you may move between them after taking every precaution.
14-699: You may cross a continuous dividing line if there is a broken line to its right.
14-700: At an unsigned intersection turning left: slow down, stop if needed, and give priority to the vehicle from the right.
14-701: Turning left with an oncoming vehicle: slow down, stop if needed, and give priority to traffic from the opposite direction.
14-702: Turning right as a vehicle approaches the intersection: slow down and continue with caution.
14-703: Going straight at an unsigned intersection with a vehicle coming: slow down and stop when necessary.
14-704: At an unsigned roundabout, give priority to the vehicle inside the roundabout coming from your left — slow down, stop if needed.
14-705: If the vehicle ahead signaled it wants to turn left, you may pass it on the right side.
14-706: When the barrier comes down at a railway crossing, stop as close as possible to the barrier.
14-707: Parking is forbidden within 2 m of a fire hydrant.
14-708: When a vehicle tries to overtake you, keep to the far right and do not speed up, so it can pass.
14-709: Continuous line with a broken line on its left: you drive to the right of the continuous line and never cross it, but oncoming traffic may.
14-710: A license holder may drive only the vehicle types recorded in their license.
14-711: On a rainy day behind a vehicle, keep a bigger distance than usual — braking is longer on a slippery road.
14-712: On an uphill road with a curb: first gear, wheels toward the middle of the road, with the handbrake.
14-713: On a steep descent, keep a bigger distance from the vehicle ahead in all cases.
14-714: Driving under the influence of alcoholic drinks is forbidden.
14-715: Driving under the influence of sedative drugs is forbidden.
14-716: The road must be used in a way that neither obstructs traffic nor endangers anyone.
14-717: The high (road) beam is used outside the cities, or when conditions allow it.
14-718: Flashing the high beam at night is allowed to signal the driver ahead that you want to overtake them.
14-719: Sudden braking is allowed only to avoid an accident.
15-720: An examiner carrying an examiner's ID from the licensing authority may inspect a vehicle and take it off the road.
15-721: If you hit a parked vehicle whose owner is absent, leave a written notice on it and inform the police.
15-722: The licensing authority may withdraw a driver's license for a set period or forever.
15-723: The duty to slow down means reducing speed until you can stop and prevent any danger.
15-724: The duty to act with caution on the road falls on every road user.
15-725: The duty to know the traffic laws and act by them falls on every road user.
15-726: A police officer carrying an examiner's ID may inspect the vehicle's fitness at any time, even with a valid license.
15-727: On a descent with a curb: wheels toward the curb, reverse gear, with the handbrake.
15-728: Any change in your health condition must be reported to the traffic department.
15-729: On a steep descent, keep a bigger distance from the vehicle ahead in all cases.
15-730: Orders of a police officer in uniform are obeyed in all cases — even against a 'No entry' sign.
15-731: If the vehicle is photographed committing a violation, the owner is responsible unless they prove someone else was driving.
15-732: The licensing authority has the power to withdraw a license, or not grant it, forever or for a set period.
15-733: Going up onto the sidewalk to pass an obstacle on the road is forbidden.
15-734: The duty to place the warning triangle applies to every motor vehicle traveling on more than two wheels.
15-735: A hand signal is permitted in all the cases mentioned (all the answers are correct).
15-736: If the accident caused material damage only, move both vehicles away and clear any hazardous material off the road.
15-737: The duty to know the traffic laws falls on everyone who uses the road.
15-738: Parking on a downhill road beside the sidewalk includes all the measures mentioned (all the answers are correct).
15-739: Parking on the left side is allowed if the road is one-way and a sign at its entrance indicates so.
15-740: At an intersection, priority goes to the vehicle from the right in all cases, when no sign says otherwise.
15-741: Responsibility for the vehicle and the passengers while driving lies with the driver.
15-742: The insurance certificate must be with the driver while driving, even if they don't own the vehicle.
15-743: Overtaking before an intersection is forbidden.
15-744: At an unsigned intersection with a vehicle from your left: you have right of way (they give way to the vehicle on their right).
15-745: The maximum speed for a private car on an urban road is up to 50 km/h.
15-746: When entering the road from a dirt road, give priority to the vehicles on the road ahead of you.
15-747: You must place the warning triangle behind the vehicle in every case where it stops to repair a fault.
15-748: In a private car outside the cities, driving faster than 80 km/h is forbidden.
15-749: Overtaking an overtaking vehicle is allowed for a commercial vehicle up to 4 tons on a road with more than two lanes in the same direction.
16-750: The warning triangle is placed behind the vehicle so a driver coming from behind sees it from at least 100 m.
16-751: On high beam outside the cities with an oncoming vehicle, dip your lights the moment you see it.
16-752: Before a crest, overtaking is forbidden in all cases when visibility is limited.
16-753: Parking on the sidewalk is forbidden unless a sign allows it.
16-754: The maximum number of passengers in a private car is seven, excluding the driver.
16-755: Signal from enough distance and time to alert the other driver before deviating or moving off.
16-756: Towing a motor vehicle with a private car is forbidden.
16-757: The owner of a private car may not transport passengers for a fare.
16-758: If your vehicle is towed from a no-parking spot, the owner of the vehicle is responsible for the violation.
16-759: If your license says glasses are required, you must wear them while driving.
16-760: A U-turn is allowed for any vehicle, provided it causes no obstruction or danger and no sign forbids it.
16-761: All the authorities mentioned may withdraw a driving license (the courts, the licensing authority...).
16-762: The person responsible for passengers wearing seat belts is the driver.
16-763: The road shoulder extends up to 3 m from the road edge, or to the drainage channel.
16-764: A road that cannot be entered from both ends is still a road in the eyes of the law.
16-765: The sidewalk is the part of the road width intended for pedestrians.
16-766: Repairing a vehicle on the road or in a public parking place is allowed if the repair is simple and not next to a workshop.
16-767: An urban road is any road with a sign at its entrance indicating entry to an urban road.
16-768: An expressway is a non-urban road for motor vehicles only, with a sign at its entrance indicating so.
16-769: A path is a road, or part of one other than the street, designated for a certain type of road user.
16-770: A two-way road is a road where travel is permitted in both directions.
16-771: Driving on the shoulder is allowed for non-motorized vehicles, tractors, and slow vehicles — to make room to be overtaken.
16-772: Stopping on the expressway shoulder is allowed only in specific cases: transporting the injured, towing, emergency and works vehicles, and breakdowns.
16-773: At an intersection, overtaking a motor vehicle is forbidden — except vehicles traveling on two wheels.
16-774: The meeting of a road with a fuel-station entrance/exit is not an intersection, unless a sign says otherwise.
16-775: If a police officer directs you into a no-entry road, you must obey the order.
16-776: The maximum permitted speed is the highest speed allowed for certain vehicles on certain roads.
16-777: The maximum speed for a private car on an expressway is up to 110 km/h.
16-778: A class (B) license holder may drive a private car and a commercial vehicle up to 4 tons.
16-779: The court can impose all the penalties mentioned on a violating driver.
17-780: Inside the cities the horn is used only to avoid an imminent danger that cannot be avoided any other way.
17-781: On a non-urban road you may stop on the shoulder to pick up and drop off passengers — except on the expressway.
17-782: Turning right: from the right side into the far right of the intended road, as far as possible.
17-783: A commercial vehicle is one used, or intended, for transporting goods.
17-784: A U-turn is allowed in all the cases mentioned (all the answers are correct).
17-785: Stopping a vehicle is forbidden within 12 m before a pedestrian crossing.
17-786: Stopping a vehicle is forbidden within 20 m of a bus-stop sign.
17-787: When parking beside the sidewalk, the distance between the nearest wheels and the sidewalk must not exceed 40 cm.
17-788: Parking a vehicle on the median is forbidden.
17-789: An expressway is non-urban, for motor vehicles only, with a sign at its entrance indicating so.
17-790: When turning left from two adjacent left-turn lanes, passing on the right is allowed.
17-791: The road shoulder is the area beside the road edge without a sidewalk, up to 3 m or to the drainage channel.
17-792: When exiting a parking area, give priority to vehicles on the road crossing in front of you.
17-793: Documents you must carry: driving license, ID, vehicle license, insurance certificate, and any certificate required by law.
17-794: Your vehicle may be driven by whoever holds a valid license for the same vehicle type and insurance covering them.
17-795: A sun visor is necessary in every motor vehicle that has a windshield.
17-796: The danger of skidding on a wet road increases the more the tires are worn smooth and unfit.
17-797: The purpose of road markings: organizing traffic and making maximum use of the road width.
17-798: If an oncoming vehicle dazzles you at night, do not use the high beam and direct your eyes to the right side of the road.
17-799: Wheel skidding is caused by several factors (speed, wet road, tires...) — all the answers are correct.
17-800: If a tire punctures, change the damaged wheel with the passengers outside the vehicle — for safety.
17-801: The reaction distance: from the moment of seeing the danger until pressing the brake pedal.
17-802: Sand on the road reduces friction between the tires and the surface and causes skidding.
17-803: If you and the vehicle ahead are at the same speed (50) in the same direction, overtaking is forbidden — pointless and dangerous.
17-804: The basic purpose of traffic lights at an intersection: organizing traffic and distributing right of way.
17-805: Overtaking is allowed only if the road conditions and traffic allow it.
17-806: Before a narrow two-way bridge without a warning sign, slow down and increase caution so you don't hit the oncoming vehicle.
17-807: The distance needed for overtaking depends on the speed of both vehicles — the one overtaking and the one being overtaken.
17-808: If the driver ahead is hesitant about which way to go, overtaking them is forbidden — dangerous.
17-809: If you see in the mirror a vehicle already overtaking you, do not overtake — keep your normal speed and let it pass.
18-810: If a vehicle comes at you in your lane from the opposite direction, slow down and keep to the far right, even onto the road margin.
18-811: If there is any doubt the vehicle ahead may swerve left, overtaking is forbidden.
18-812: If the sun dazzles you behind a vehicle, drive at a speed that suits your range of vision.
18-813: If you feel a puncture, press the brakes gradually and slow down until the vehicle stops.
18-814: The braking distance grows the faster you drive.
18-815: On a wet road with water-filled holes, slow down and drive at a reasonable speed for the conditions.
18-816: On a wet road the vehicle is more likely to skid the more the speed increases.
18-817: Before starting to drive it is important to check everything (mirrors, brakes, surroundings...) — all the answers are correct.
18-818: On a road section with oil, slow down a lot and try not to use the brakes — so you don't skid.
18-819: At night the reaction time is longer because visibility is unclear.
18-820: When reversing and turning the steering wheel left, the rear of the vehicle moves to the left.
18-821: If a vehicle is stopped inside a busy intersection, overtaking is absolutely forbidden.
18-822: If you entered on green and it turned yellow, keep going and clear the intersection carefully and as quickly as possible.
18-823: On a road that is not clear for a sufficient distance, overtaking is forbidden.
18-824: In fog outside the cities, drive at a speed that suits your range of vision.
18-825: You may cross the broken line only if the traffic requirements make it necessary.
18-826: At an intersection full of vehicles, stop outside the intersection until it clears.
18-827: After completing an overtake on a two-way road, return to the right lane after keeping a sufficient distance.
18-828: If someone ignores your right of way at the intersection, slow down, stop, and give up your right — safety comes first.
18-829: The stopping distance needed at night is greater than during the day — visibility is lower.
18-830: Green is not an absolute duty — the driver may judge according to what is happening at the intersection.
18-831: With a flashing green light, the driver may judge whether to stop or cross, according to the situation.
18-832: When overtaking you must consider speed, distance, and the road — all the answers are correct.
18-833: If the vehicle ahead signaled to turn left, you may pass it on its right carefully if the road is wide enough.
18-834: At an intersection full of vehicles, slow down to let the vehicles clear the intersection.
18-835: Speed is limited on all roads, including the expressway — no road is without a speed limit.
18-836: The broken dividing line allows overtaking the cart only if the road is clear.
18-837: On a single-lane uphill road with an oncoming truck, you have priority, but it is best to be considerate of the truck.
18-838: If you feel the vehicle approaching the intersection won't give you priority, slow down and even stop until it clears.
18-839: On a downhill road, drive slowly using a low gear — to keep control of the vehicle.
19-840: If you stopped on a street and want to continue, give priority to all vehicles on the street — you are joining the traffic.
19-841: When stopping the vehicle and getting out, use the handbrake in all cases.
19-842: When visibility is limited, drive slowly and match your speed to your range of vision.
19-843: To overtake on a clear road, go faster than the vehicle you're overtaking, without exceeding the maximum speed.
19-844: On a rough road, slow down — to avoid difficulties.
19-845: The stopping distance depends on several factors (speed, road, brakes...) — all the answers are correct.
19-846: The braking distance: from the moment of pressing the brake pedal until the complete stop.
19-847: The stopping distance: from the moment of seeing the danger until the complete stop.
19-848: The driver's reaction distance: from seeing the danger until pressing the brake pedal.
19-849: Where there are holes and potholes, slow down enough to be able to stop when approaching them.
19-850: Traffic consists of three elements: the road, the driver, and the vehicle.
19-851: The braking distance: from the moment you press the brake pedal until the complete stop.
19-852: Pushing a vehicle with its engine off into a road closed to motor vehicles is absolutely forbidden.
19-853: You know a road is one-way if a sign at its entrance says so, or if it is divided along its length by a median.
19-854: At night you know whether you're on high or low beam by a special light on the instrument panel.
19-855: Too much air pressure in the tire puts great strain on the fabric and rubber layers.
19-856: The engine's air filter has several functions (cleaning the air entering the engine...) — all the answers are correct.
19-857: If the engine's cooling water runs low, the water temperature in the engine rises.
19-858: Tire wear in the middle only is caused by air pressure higher than necessary.
19-859: The cooling cycle's job is keeping the engine temperature between 70-90 degrees Celsius.
19-860: If the dipstick shows oil above the upper mark, it means water or fuel is leaking into the oil.
19-861: Incorrect wheel balancing causes rapid tire wear.
19-862: Acid (white powder) forming on the battery terminals must be cleaned with water so the connection stays proper.
19-863: Driving a vehicle whose engine has no oil causes engine damage.
19-864: The gearbox's job is matching the engine's rotation speed with the wheels' rotation speed.
19-865: You check the radiator water by opening the radiator cap while the engine is cold.
19-866: The ammeter indicates the charging or discharging of the battery.
19-867: Low air pressure in the tires damages the tires on the sides.
19-868: You must check the oil level in the oil sump every day.
19-869: The gearbox matches the engine's rotation with the wheels' rotation.
20-870: If the water temperature rises and you want to add water, open the radiator cap carefully with the engine running.
20-871: A sudden drop in oil pressure means a fault in the oil pump.
20-872: We make sure there is enough air in the tires every day.
20-873: With sudden braking, the vehicle still travels a certain distance before it stops.
20-874: If the brakes fail, try to stop the vehicle with the handbrake.
20-875: If one front tire has lower pressure than the other, the steering pulls toward the lower-pressure wheel.
20-876: If a tire punctures, driving on it is forbidden.
20-877: You check the tires daily with a simple look at them.
20-878: The spare wheel's air pressure follows the manufacturer's instructions.
20-879: You always press the accelerator pedal with the right foot.
20-880: You always press the brake pedal with the right foot.
20-881: You always press the clutch pedal with the left foot.
20-882: You check the sump oil by inserting a special rod (dipstick) into the oil pan.
20-883: The windshield washer helps remove dirt and clean the glass.
20-884: The windshield defogger works with hot air blown onto the inner side of the glass.
20-885: After crossing a road flooded with water, the braking system is the one likely to be affected.
20-886: Incorrect wheel balancing causes rapid tire wear.
20-887: Shifting gears without pressing the clutch (in a manual vehicle) can break the cogwheels in the gearbox.
20-888: If the engine won't start and there is a strong fuel smell, the carburetor is flooded with fuel.
20-889: To check the oil level in the sump, park the vehicle on level ground.
20-890: If the front tires don't have enough air pressure, steering right or left becomes difficult.
20-891: You must check the distilled water level inside the battery once a week.
20-892: The danger of skidding on a wet road increases the more the tires are worn smooth and unfit.
20-893: When lifting the vehicle to change a wheel, make sure it can't move and use the handbrake.
20-894: Using the vehicle is forbidden if the brake light doesn't work — until it is repaired.
20-895: The handbrake is used when parking and in emergencies.
20-896: If you suspect the foot brake can't stop the vehicle, don't drive it until it is repaired.
20-897: If you feel a puncture, press the brakes lightly so the vehicle stops gradually.
20-898: If the right headlight fails at night, continuing with one headlight is forbidden.
20-899: If the right headlight fails at night, continuing to drive with one headlight is forbidden.
21-900: The carburetor's job is mixing air with fuel to make a combustible mixture that runs the engine.
21-901: The choke's job is making a fuel-rich mixture to help start the engine in cold weather.
21-902: The fuel-air mixture that runs the engine is formed in the carburetor.
21-903: When you press the clutch, the engine doesn't transmit power to move the wheels.
21-904: If the electric spark doesn't reach the spark plugs, the engine doesn't work.
21-905: If tire pressure is higher than required, the vehicle becomes more sensitive to vibrations.
21-906: Wheel-change order: loosen the bolts slightly, lift the vehicle, then unscrew the bolts completely.
21-907: Tire air pressure follows the manufacturer's instructions.
21-908: If the gasoline engine stops suddenly, first check for a fault in the electric current.
21-909: The battery's job is storing the electric current.
21-910: The oil pressure gauge/lamp shows the oil pressure level in the engine while it runs.
21-911: If the sump oil is low, add the missing amount at the nearest fuel station.
21-912: If the steering feels different from before, go straight to the garage to repair it.
21-913: You notice the fan belt snapped when the ammeter lights up and the engine water temperature rises.
21-914: Incorrect tire pressure wears the tires out quickly — correct.
21-915: The speedometer shows the vehicle's speed in km/h.
21-916: The oil pressure gauge shows whether oil reaches all the moving parts in the engine.
21-917: If the carburetor is flooded, wait a bit until the fuel evaporates, then start the engine.
21-918: The fuel gauge shows the amount of fuel in the tank.
21-919: The light-switching device lets the driver change the high beam to the low beam and back.
21-920: The sump oil must be changed after a number of kilometers, per the manufacturer's instructions.
21-921: The lights warning lamp on the instrument panel lights up when we use the high beam.
21-922: In modern vehicles, the brake system lamp lights up when brake fluid is low.
21-2095: The electronic stability system (E.S.P) corrects the driver's braking errors, prevents skidding, and increases the vehicle's stability.
21-2096: The head restraint reduces the head's backward whipping during a collision — it protects your neck.
21-2097: Seat belts, head restraints, and airbags reduce the severity of passenger injuries in an accident.
21-2098: To move from (P) to a driving position in an automatic: press the brake and the release button on the lever, then shift as needed.
21-2099: The handbrake doesn't act on all the wheels and its power is weak, so it is used for parking only.
21-2100: The ABS system lets you keep control of the vehicle and the steering while braking.
21-2101: Bumpers matter because they reduce the damage to the vehicle and its passengers in an accident.
22-2102: The ABS keeps your ability to steer and maneuver even during emergency braking.
22-2103: The electronic stability system (E.S.P) reduces the danger of the vehicle skidding.
22-2104: The E.S.P applies the brakes on each wheel individually and controls engine power in dangerous situations.
22-2105: The stability system (E.S.P) helps keep the vehicle balanced during skidding and rollover danger.
22-2106: Installing hard, protruding accessories in the cabin is forbidden because they injure passengers in an accident.
22-2107: Even with airbags, it is essential to wear the seat belt at every moment — the airbag complements it, not replaces it.
22-2108: The tire's production date is printed on the tire itself.
22-2109: A fast-flashing indicator lamp means one of the indicator bulbs isn't working or is unfit.
22-2110: When replacing a bulb, choose a suitable bulb per the vehicle manufacturer's instructions.
22-2111: The danger of an exhaust leak: burnt gases enter the cabin and cause dizziness/poisoning.
22-2112: You reduce air pollution from the exhaust with regular maintenance of the engine and its systems.
22-2113: The GPS gives you information about the vehicle's location and helps you reach your destination.
22-2114: Place the jack at the spot marked by the manufacturer, or another safe spot near the wheel you're changing.
22-2115: Uncalibrated headlights don't light the road as required.
22-2116: If the handbrake lamp lights up while driving, the handbrake is up and must be released — it is actually braking the vehicle.
22-2117: With ABS brakes, pressing hard causes pedal vibration and noise — that is normal.
22-2118: A hybrid vehicle has an electric motor in addition to the regular engine.
22-2119: One advantage of the hybrid vehicle is lower fuel consumption than a regular vehicle.
22-2120: After parking: handbrake, the appropriate gear, engine off, and take the key out.
22-2121: The speedometer must be in sound condition at all times.
22-2122: The rear fog lamp can be turned on only when the headlights are on.
22-2123: To secure a parked automatic vehicle: use the handbrake and move the lever to park (P).
22-2124: Before moving the gear lever to start driving, press the brake pedal to keep the vehicle from moving.
22-2125: The rear camera reveals the blind spot that forms behind the vehicle.
22-2126: Proper use of the seat belt reduces the injury of the person wearing it in an accident.
22-2127: In the interior mirror you must see the surroundings beyond the rear window as much as possible.
22-2128: Before starting the engine: foot brake pressed, handbrake up, gear in neutral, clutch pressed (and in an automatic - P).
22-2129: The correct head restraint: its upper edge level with the top of the driver's head — to protect you in a collision.
22-2130: Before starting the engine, make sure the handbrake is up and working.
22-2131: Before shifting from (P) to another gear in an automatic, press hard on the brake pedal.
23-2132: Seat belts, airbags, and head restraints reduce the severity of passenger injuries in an accident.
23-2133: The braking signal is given by the brake light coming on (the red rear lamps).
23-2134: If the oil pressure lamp lights up while driving, stop the vehicle immediately and turn off the engine — so it doesn't get damaged.
23-2135: First thing after entering the vehicle and before driving: adjust the seat, steering wheel, mirrors, and head restraint.
23-2136: One of the most important advantages of hybrid vehicles is lower air pollution and protecting the environment.
23-2137: The 'check engine' indicator means there is a fault inside the engine.
23-2138: Diesel exhaust fluid (AdBlue) is a solution of urea and deionized water.
23-2139: AdBlue is used in diesel vehicles only.
23-2140: AdBlue's importance: it reduces the toxic gases coming out of the exhaust pipe.
23-2141: You know AdBlue has run out through a special indicator on the instrument panel.
23-2142: When AdBlue runs out, engine power may drop or the engine may stop, depending on the manufacturer.
23-2143: A 'check engine' fault can be in one of the engine systems (ignition, fuel, emissions, exhaust).
23-2144: If 'check engine' appears flashing, there is a major engine fault — take the vehicle to the service center.
23-2145: You reduce air pollution by keeping the engine working properly per the manufacturer's instructions and correct tire pressure.
23-2146: You reduce pollution by filling with the appropriate fuel and periodic vehicle maintenance.
23-2147: Driving downhill in neutral is forbidden and very dangerous — you lose control of the vehicle.
23-2148: In the side mirrors you must see the side edge of the vehicle and the road on both its sides.
23-2149: The danger of refueling with the engine running: fuel vapor can ignite from a spark.
